text.title: Cloudflare Pages + GitHub Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `Cloudflare Pages + GitHub` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Production branch MUST be set explicitly (not the GitHub default). Implicit-production = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Accidentally merging a feature branch to GitHub default while production branch is unconfigured silently ships an unexpected revision to prod.

rule.statement: Build-time secrets (API keys, DSNs) MUST live in Pages project env, not in committed code or repo secrets. Mixed storage = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Committed env vars are public on the repo; Pages env is isolated per environment (production / preview).

rule.statement: Operator MUST run `wrangler pages dev` locally and verify the build before first deploy. Skip = REJECT for first deploy.
rule.rationale: First cloud deploy without local verify wastes 5-10 minutes per round-trip and burns Pages quotas debugging.

rule.statement: Preview deploys MUST be enabled for every PR. Disabled previews = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Preview URLs are the entire reason to use Pages over a simpler static host; turning them off removes the value.

rule.statement: Custom domain MUST attach via CNAME + Full(Strict) SSL. Plain Full or Flexible = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Same loop hazard as the parent DNS methodology; Pages issues edge certs but origin must validate.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume cycles and produce false confidence.
